scope.summary:
Goal: Implement the foundational text buffer and syntax highlighting layer for the native code editor panel. This is the root MT that all other E1 MTs build on. Expected diff shape: new Rust module `src/frontend/code_editor/buffer.rs` (ropey-based text buffer with edit/query API), new module `src/frontend/code_editor/highlight.rs` (tree-sitter highlight pipeline), new `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs` wiring them, and integration into the egui panel struct at `src/frontend/panels/code_editor_panel.rs`.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs` â€” declares buffer and highlight submodules, exports the top-level `CodeEditorPanel` egui widget struct.
- CREATE `src/frontend/code_editor/buffer.rs` â€” wraps the `ropey` crate `Rope` type. Exposes: `TextBuffer::new(text: &str) -> Self`, `insert(byte_offset: usize, text: &str)`, `delete(byte_range: Range<usize>)`, `slice_to_string(line_range: Range<usize>) -> String`, `len_bytes() -> usize`, `len_lines() -> usize`, `line_to_byte(line_idx: usize) -> usize`, `byte_to_line(byte_offset: usize) -> usize`. The buffer is the sole owner of document text; all other subsystems (highlight, folding, minimap) borrow slices from it.
- CREATE `src/frontend/code_editor/highlight.rs` â€” uses `tree-sitter` + language grammars (start with `tree-sitter-rust` and `tree-sitter-javascript` as bundled crates; add more via a `LanguageRegistry` map keyed on file extension). Exposes `Highlighter::new(language: Language) -> Self`, `highlight(source: &[u8]) -> Vec<HighlightSpan>` where `HighlightSpan { byte_range: Range<usize>, scope: HighlightScope }`. `HighlightScope` is an enum: `Keyword`, `String`, `Comment`, `Number`, `Function`, `Type`, `Operator`, `Other`.
- EDIT `src/frontend/panels/code_editor_panel.rs` (create if not yet existing) â€” mounts a `CodeEditorPanel` egui widget: renders visible lines from the buffer, applies highlight spans as colored text runs via `egui::RichText`, provides a stable `egui::Id` for the panel, and sets AccessKit metadata.

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `ropey = "1.6"` â€” battle-tested rope data structure; handles large files and incremental edits cheaply.
- `tree-sitter = "0.22"` + `tree-sitter-rust = "0.21"` + `tree-sitter-javascript = "0.21"` â€” canonical grammar crates; parse incrementally using `Parser::parse_with` and the old tree for re-use.
- `egui` text rendering: `ui.painter().galley(...)` or `ui.label(egui::RichText::new(text).color(color))` per span.
- `accesskit` â€” set `NodeBuilder::new(Role::TextInput)` with `author_id` = `"code_editor_buffer"` on the outer panel node.

React reference: `app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx` â€” the Monaco-based code block that this native surface replaces. Study the language field, readOnly flag, and status-dispatch pattern. Note that Monaco handled highlighting internally; here tree-sitter replaces it.
Backend: No direct backend call in this MT. The buffer is purely in-process. Backend code-nav APIs are wired in MT-008.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Add `ropey`, `tree-sitter`, `tree-sitter-rust`, `tree-sitter-javascript` to `src/frontend/Cargo.toml` (or the workspace `Cargo.toml` if frontend is a workspace member).
2. Implement `TextBuffer` in `buffer.rs`. Keep all methods panic-free for out-of-range inputs (clamp or return `Err`).
3. Implement `Highlighter` in `highlight.rs`. Parse the full buffer on first call; on subsequent calls pass the previous tree as `old_tree` for incremental re-parse. Map tree-sitter `Highlight` indices to `HighlightScope` using a fixed lookup table against the `highlights.scm` capture names (e.g. `keyword` -> `Keyword`, `string` -> `String`).
4. Implement `CodeEditorPanel::show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui)` in `mod.rs`. For now render all lines (virtualization is MT-002). Use a `egui::ScrollArea` for vertical scroll. Each line: iterate spans overlapping the line, split text into colored runs, render with `egui::RichText`. Use the theme token `visuals.code_bg_color` for the background.
5. Wire `CodeEditorPanel` into the egui tile layout (WP-011 docking) so it appears as a named tile.
6. Add an AccessKit `author_id = "code_editor_panel"` `Role::GenericContainer` on the outer `Frame` and `author_id = "code_editor_text"` `Role::TextInput` on the inner text area so swarm agents can address the widget.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: None â€” this is the root MT. Requires `ropey` and `tree-sitter` crates available in Cargo workspace.
Risk if missed: All other E1 MTs are blocked; no text foundation means no virtualization, no multi-cursor, no find/replace, no folding.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend` passes with tests for TextBuffer insert/delete/slice covering at least: empty buffer, single-line insert, multi-line insert, delete spanning lines, byte-to-line and line-to-byte round-trips.
AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend` passes with a test that parses a 10-line Rust snippet through Highlighter and returns at least one span with scope=Keyword and one with scope=Function.
AC-003: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend` passes with a test that parses a JS snippet and returns at least one span with scope=String.

AC-004: An egui_kittest screenshot of the CodeEditorPanel rendering a 5-line Rust snippet shows colored text (at least two distinct foreground colors present, verified by pixel sampling of the test image).
AC-005: The AccessKit tree exported by egui_kittest contains a node with author_id='code_editor_panel' and role=GenericContainer and a child node with author_id='code_editor_text' and role=TextInput.
AC-006: No `unwrap()` calls on buffer range operations in production code paths â€” all fallible operations return `Option` or `Result`.
proof_targets: PT-001: Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend text_buffer` â€” all tests GREEN, output logged.
PT-002: Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend highlighter` â€” all tests GREEN, output logged.
PT-003: Run egui_kittest screenshot test `code_editor_panel_basic` â€” screenshot saved to `test_output/MT-001-panel-basic.png`, pixel check passes.
PT-004: Run egui_kittest AccessKit dump test `code_editor_panel_accesskit` â€” JSON output contains both required author_ids.
implementation_notes: Use `ropey::Rope` not `String` from the start â€” retrofitting later when files are large is expensive.
tree-sitter incremental re-parse: always pass `Some(&old_tree)` to `parser.parse_with` after the first parse to get O(edit) re-parse cost.
For highlight colors, define a `fn scope_to_color(scope: HighlightScope, visuals: &egui::Visuals) -> egui::Color32` function â€” keep theme-aware, not hardcoded hex.
Do not call `.to_string()` on the full rope for every frame â€” slice only the visible range (even before MT-002 virtualization, limit to first 1000 lines as a guard).
Wrap tree-sitter `Language` in a `Send + Sync` newtype if the egui event loop requires it.
HighlightScope must derive Clone, Debug, PartialEq for tests.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx â€” language field, readOnly flag, status-dispatch pattern, language picker data model
app/src/lib/monaco/language_registry.ts â€” HANDSHAKE_CODE_LANGUAGES list: port the same language IDs to a Rust LanguageRegistry map (language_id -> tree-sitter Language)
binds_backend_api: None in this MT. Buffer is in-process only. Backend code-nav APIs are wired in MT-008.

risks: RISK-001: tree-sitter grammar crates lag behind the upstream tree-sitter-rust version and may not compile against the same `tree-sitter` core version. Resolve by pinning both in lockfile and checking `cargo tree -d` for duplicates.

RISK-002: ropey's byte-offset API vs char-offset confusion causes off-by-one bugs in highlight span alignment. Mitigate by always converting highlight byte ranges via `Rope::byte_to_char` before rendering.
RISK-003: Rendering all lines without virtualization makes startup with large files (>10 k lines) block the egui frame. Guard with a 1000-line render cap until MT-002 lands.
RISK-004: AccessKit author_id collisions if multiple CodeEditorPanel instances are mounted (e.g. diff view). Use panel-instance uuid suffix appended to the author_id string.
RISK-005: tree-sitter `Language` is not `Send` in some versions â€” will fail if egui moves tasks to threads. Wrap in `Arc<Mutex<...>>` preemptively.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Unit test all TextBuffer range operations with both in-bounds and out-of-bounds inputs â€” confirm no panic.
MC-002: CI cargo test gate on the frontend crate must include MT-001 tests before any downstream MT is marked READY.
MC-003: Screenshot test must run in CI (headless wgpu or software rasterizer) and artifact the PNG.
MC-004: Cargo.lock must be committed so grammar crate versions are reproducible across machines.
